questions.question: What is the algorithm of algorithms?
questions.correct_answer: A single principle that generates all other thinking algorithms - a meta-algorithm that creates algorithms and is capable of generating itself and all other algorithms.

questions.question: According to the lesson, what is the simplest operation that can generate all of thought?
questions.correct_answer: The act of distinction itself - the drawing of a boundary. The minimal operation is: Make a distinction, Observe the distinction, See the observer, Repeat.

questions.question: What does closure mean in the context of the algorithm of algorithms?
questions.correct_answer: Closure means the algorithm can be applied to its own output, the system of operations is complete, and nothing external is required to sustain the process.

questions.question: What makes the algorithm of algorithms universal?
questions.correct_answer: It can implement any other algorithm and any other system of thought, and can be embodied across different domains like neural networks, digital computers, social systems, and biological evolution.

questions.question: After exploring all the complexity, what does the lesson say the algorithm of algorithms ultimately is?
questions.correct_answer: Make a distinction. Everything else - recursion, self-reference, universality, closure - follows from this single operation applied to itself.
